segments.text: The richest man in Europe bought a company that everyone thought was finished. [music] But he turned it into a $200 billion empire. In the 1980s,
Dior wasn't Dior. It was this wounded animal limping around Paris. But there was one man that saw the potential in
it. Bernard Arno. Arno had this vision that Dior could be a part of this stable of luxury brands. And he thought that
over the next 50 years, this luxury brand portfolio could [music] be something incredibly special. The thing
was, everyone thought he was crazy, but sure enough, in the 1980s, [music] he acquired Dior, and over the last 40
years, he's gone and acquired 50 other luxury brands and [music] turned it into a portfolio worth over $200 billion. And
so, this is the thing, right? There's not much of a line in the early days of building something between looking absolutely [music] crazy and having a compelling vision.
